name: New England Clam Chowder IV
recipeInstructions:
1. Place bacon in a deep, 2-quart, heat-resistant, non-metallic casserole.
2. Heat, covered with a paper towel, in Microwave Oven for 4 minutes or until
bacon is crisp.
3. Remove cooked bacon with a slotted spoon. Crumble bacon and set aside.
Reserve drippings in casserole.
4. Drain liquid from clams and add liquid to bacon drippings. Set clams aside.
5. Add potatoes, onion, and carrot to casserole.
6. Heat, covered, in Microwave Oven for 8 minutes or until vegetables are
tender; stir occasionally.
7. Blend flour into vegetable mixture.
8. Gradually stir in milk until smooth.
9. Heat, uncovered, in Microwave Oven for 4 minutes or until thickened and
smooth.
10. Stir in cream, salt, pepper, thyme, and reserved clams.
11. Heat, uncovered, in Microwave Oven for 4 minutes or until heated through.
12. Garnish with crumbled bacon before serving.
